title: Krane (Rebel Alliance)
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]]|the [[Krane|Imperial officer]]}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:AdmiralKrane2.jpg]] |name=Krane |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him{{LucasArts|url=products/rebel2/reb_stry.htm|text=''Rebel Assault II'' - Original Story}} |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Gray]] |feathers= |eyes=Dark |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Pay attention kid, this is Admiral Krane. Due to the importance of this mission, I'm training you myself. Stay alert.''"
"''I copy. Should be just like back home.''"
"''Don't be getting cocky on me, kid. You're doin' okay, but you're not in a fancy X-wing fighter anymore.|Admiral Krane and Rookie One, before TIE fighter training|audio=Krane1-RAII.flac|}} '''Krane''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and held the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]]. An uncompromising [[Alliance Military/Legends|officer]] with a fierce loathing of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Krane felt that the Alliance could only be successful in the war by deploying a skilled, capable fighting force. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Krane personally instructed the [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]] "[[Rookie One]]" in the operation of a captured Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]], ahead of the young [[pilot]]'s [[Mission to Imdaar|mission]] to steal one of the Empire's new [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE phantom]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. Krane escorted Rookie One on the first part of his journey to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Imdaar/Legends|Imdaar]] and helped to defeat a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]], ensuring Rookie One's safe arrival. ==Biography== ===Training Rookie One=== {{Quote|If you're gonna make it through okay, you gotta learn to think like the Imperial bucketheads… just follow my lead and do as I say.|Admiral Krane, to Rookie One|audio=Krane2-RAII.flac|}} [[File:AdmiralKrane.jpg|thumb|left|240px|Krane pilots a TIE fighter to instruct Rookie One with the craft's operation.]] A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Krane became a member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and, in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'' to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. held the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]]. In the [[Standard month/Legends|months]] following the Empire's [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|assault]] on the [[Echo Base/Legends|Rebel base]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], Alliance [[Starship/Legends|ships]] were subject to attacks from a new Imperial weapon, the [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE/ph phantom]], a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] equipped with a [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]], which allowed it to attack unseen. [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Intelligence]] reports indicated a high level of [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial naval]] activity near the planet [[Imdaar/Legends|Imdaar]] in the [[Dantus sector/Legends|Dantus sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]],{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Imdaar system/Legends|Imdaar system]] and that the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] ''[[Terror]]'' was en route to deploy a full [[Fighter wing/Legends|wing]] of the new starfighters. The Alliance planned to send the [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] known as "[[Rookie One]]" to Imdaar, where he was to meet with Rebel agent [[Ru Murleen]], infiltrate the ''Terror'', and capture a TIE phantom for study. In preparation for the [[Mission to Imdaar|mission]], Rookie One was sent for training on [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] starfighters. Due to the importance of the mission, Krane elected to personally instruct the young pilot. Piloting two of the few functioning [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] captured by the Alliance, Krane and Rookie One made their way to a narrow canyon shrouded in mist and filled with tight turns and rocky outcroppings. Krane was impressed when Rookie One successfully followed his lead though the canyon with minimal damage to the [[Deflector shield/Legends|unshielded]] starfighter, even opting to take the more hazardous route when the canyon forked. ===Mission to Imdaar=== {{Quote|Well, kid. I didn't think you had it in you.''"
"''Thank you, Sir.''"
"''Looks like the Alliance owes you a debt of gratitude. Without the advantage of their phantom fleet, the Empire will have to face us on equal ground. Once again, you've saved us all.|Admiral Krane and Rookie One|audio=Krane3-RAII.flac|}} [[File:KraneYWing-RAII.png|thumb|right|250px|Krane escorts Rookie One to Imdaar in a Y-wing.]] Following the training, Krane, along with [[Darnell Reggs]], escorted Rookie One on the first part of his journey to Imdaar in a three-ship [[Flight group/Legends|flight group]] of [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing starfighters]]. Along the way, the trio was [[Starfighter combat/Legends|attacked]] by over a dozen Imperial [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]]. Despite being heavily outnumbered, the Rebel pilots were able to destroy the Imperial fighters and escape without loss. Since a lone starfighter stood a greater chance of slipping past Imperial defenses, Krane and Reggs broke off and returned to base, leaving Rookie One to proceed alone to the planet [[Ceti 597]], and from there to Imdaar. Rookie One and Murleen were successful in not only capturing a TIE phantom but destroying both the [[Imdaar Alpha research station|facility]] that was manufacturing the starfighters and the ''Terror'' itself. The pair arrived at the rendezvous point in the stolen starfighter, where they were met by Krane upon landing. The admiral thanked the pair for effectively ending the threat of the TIE phantoms and presented Rookie One with a medal. However, the capture of the TIE phantom proved short-lived, as it [[Self-destruct mechanism/Legends|self-destructed]] soon after arriving at the Rebel facility. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|You're surprising me, kid. Now let's see if you're up to it.|Admiral Krane, to Rookie One|audio=Krane4-RAII.flac|}} [[File:KraneSaysHi-RAII.png|thumb|left|250px|Krane greets Rookie One and Ru Murleen after their mission to Imdaar.]] Krane was a seasoned [[Alliance Military/Legends|Rebel officer]] who harbored an intense hatred toward the Galactic Empire. A capable pilot, he could operate both Rebel and Imperial starfighters and was involved in training other pilots in their use. In battle, Krane was quick to organize his [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmates]] and ensured that they worked as a team when facing superior numbers. Believing that the Alliance could only be victorious with a well-trained and capable military, he kept those under his command on a tight rein and demanded the highest levels of performance. As a result, he was seen as a hard man lacking in interpersonal skills. Krane had dark [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], light skin, and [[Color/Legends|gray]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] during the Galactic Civil War. ==Equipment== When he greeted Rookie One and Ru Murleen following their successful mission to Imdaar, Krane wore a tan [[Alliance military uniform/Legends|Alliance officer's uniform]] and [[cloak]]. In combat, he wore a [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]] and [[Flight helmet/Legends|helmet]]. ==Behind the scenes== ===Gameplay alternatives=== {{Quote|Krane to base, I'm coming in. You gotta stop sending me these kids. I'm running out of TIE fighters.|Admiral Krane, from a game-over cutscene|audio=Krane5-RAII.flac|}} [[File:Krane_on_set.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Craig C. Lewis filming in front of a blue screen on the ''Rebel Assault II'' set]] Krane was created for the [[1995]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'', in which he was portrayed by [[Craig C. Lewis]]. During the level "TIE Training," the player is given a choice of taking the easier route through the canyon or following Krane on the more difficult path. Krane's subsequent dialogue varies depending on the player's choice, remarking that he is surprised if Rookie One opts to take the difficult route. If Rookie One is [[Death/Legends|killed]] during the level, the game-over cutscene shows Krane returning to base, telling the Rebels to stop bringing reckless kids to him, as they are running out of TIE fighters. The way Krane congratulates Rookie One at the end of the game depends on the difficulty level chosen by the player. At "beginner" and "novice" levels, Krane offers Rookie One a handshake while remarking that he had undertaken more difficult assignments. At the "standard" difficulty level, he presents Rookie One with a medal, while at "expert" difficulty he presents a more elaborate medal. This article assumes the player takes the more difficult path during "TIE Training" and plays at "expert" level. ===Character development=== [[File:GoldLeaderHelmet-SWC.jpg|thumb|left|210px|Jon Vander's helmet from ''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] Project leader [[Vincent Lee]]'s storyboards and scripts document for ''Rebel Assault II'', dated [[November 16]], [[1994]], included a brief description of Krane,''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]'' much of which was included in a short biography of the character on the LucasArts website. However, the character's age of "late forties" was not mentioned in the game, its manual, or online and was only reprinted in [[Rob Smith]]'s [[2008]] book, ''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]''. Lee also made notes on the people he thought symbolized each character in the game, selecting actor [[Wikipedia:Tommy Lee Jones|Tommy Lee Jones]] to represent Krane. Both Lee's notes and the LucasArts website biography refer to him as "[[Commander/Legends|Commander]] Krane," though the character is called "Admiral Krane" in the game's dialogue and end credits. Props and costumes created for the [[original trilogy]] of ''[[Star Wars]]'' movies were used during the filming of ''Rebel Assault II''.{{LucasArts|url=products/rebel2/reb_vid.htm|text=''Rebel Assault II'' - Live Action Video}} The flight helmet worn by Krane while piloting a Y-wing is the same design as that originally worn by [[Gold Leader/Legends|Gold Leader]]''[[Star Wars Costumes: The Original Trilogy]]'' [[Jon Vander/Legends|Jon "Dutch" Vander]]{{CSWECite|III|293|Vander, Jon}} in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Krane|imagecat=Images of Krane}} *{{AdventurerCite|10|Rebel Assault II: Lights, Camera, Action|''Rebel Assault II'': Lights, Camera, Action}} {{Po}} *{{GalaxyCite|5|Game Room|Game Room: Rebel Rouser}} {{Po}} *{{LucasArts|url=products/rebel2/reb_stry.htm|text=''Rebel Assault II'' - Original Story}} *''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Krane }} [[Category:Alliance Fleet admirals]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pilots]]
